shared_groups: International Transfer Restrictions
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor does not transfer personal data to third countries or international organizations, matching the regulation's transfer prohibition
results.condition_check: compatible: contract is an unconditional prohibition, which is at least as strict as the regulation's prohibition absent prior controller authorization
results.modality_check: compatible: 'does not' is a prohibitive/restrictive formulation equal-or-greater in force than 'shall not'
results.other_constraints_check: no additional timing/manner/location/cause constraints; contract is not looser
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
Data Subject Rights And Supervisory Authority Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The processor is obligated to assist the controller with DPIA-related compliance; the contract requires the processor to assist the controller in preparing DPIAs and related prior consultation under GDPR Articles 35 and 36, which is the same or a broader assistance obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds a limiting qualifier ('if necessary and relevant'), which is a reasonable and expected condition and does not make the duty weaker than the regulation's unconditional assistance requirement in a way that defeats the core obligation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall', matching the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's scope is at least as broad, covering DPIA preparation and prior consultation under Articles 35 and 36, which aligns with or exceeds assistance in ensuring compliance with DPIA obligations.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor must ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract requires processor ensure its employees processing personal data do so only in accordance with the Instruction
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall on contract matches regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: Equivalent substance; contract is at least as strict, with employees under processor's authority and instruction-based processing
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the personal data breach notification to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects, and the contract sub-item requires a description of the measures the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including measures to mitigate adverse effects. Same substantive content, same axis (measures taken/proposed to mitigate effects), with the parent clause making it mandatory.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Contract sub-item is a plain descriptive statement within a mandatory parent clause ('shall ... include the following'), so it carries mandatory force equal to or greater than the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Substantively equivalent timing/manner/target/location/cause; no weaker constraints.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor to process personal data, but the contract ties this to post-termination continuation rather than processing on documented instructions from the controller
results.condition_check: not aligned: regulation requires documented instructions from the controller; contract requires necessity and applicability under law, plus a three-month post-termination period
results.modality_check: same force (shall)
results.other_constraints_check: timing is different and narrower in scope (up to three months after termination) and does not match the regulation's instruction-based condition
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance

shared_groups: International Transfer Restrictions

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation concerns a processor's liability for damage caused by processing under GDPR Article 82(2), while the contract limits each party's cumulated liability to payments under the Main Agreement; different obligation and different object.

If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

results.condition_check: not aligned: regulation is triggered by an instruction infringing GDPR or other data protection provisions; contract is conditioned on processing the controller’s personal data and a written request for documentation
results.modality_check: contract is a plain statement within a mandatory parent, but it does not impose the required informing duty
results.other_constraints_check: timing and manner are missing; no immediate notice to the controller is provided
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with the Applicable Law in respect of the processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors. The contract instead requires providing documentation of monitoring and control of sub-processors, which is a different assistance/information duty and does not cover notice of intended sub-processor changes.
results.condition_check: Different condition: regulation applies in case of general written authorization; contract applies only if documentation is requested in writing.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), so modality is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant match on timing/manner/target/location/cause; the contract's documentation-on-request duty is substantively different from advance notice of intended changes.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.
